name: Resistance Band Squat
resistance_profile: Ascending band tension makes the top of the squat hardest, biasing the shortened position.
cues: Stand tall to feel the band tension peak
Sit down and back with knees over the toes
Keep the band anchored under your mid-foot
execution_steps: Stand on the middle of a long resistance band with both feet, shoulder-width apart.
Bring the band up and hold the ends at shoulder height in a front-rack style, or loop it over your shoulders.
Brace your core and sit down and back into a squat until the thighs reach at least parallel.
Drive through the feet to stand tall, where band tension peaks, and squeeze the quads at the top.
Control the descent and keep tension on the band throughout.
good_when: Training at home or travelling with minimal equipment
You want extra resistance at the top of the squat
You need a joint-friendly, low-load quad option
bad_when: You need heavy loading for a maximal strength or hypertrophy stimulus
You want the most resistance in the deep, stretched position
common_errors: Letting the band slip out from under the feet
Rushing the top and losing tension
Using too light a band to actually challenge the quads
progressions: Use a thicker band or double up bands
Slow the tempo or add a pause
